channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Not Coming To Nintendo Switch At Launch
item.content:encoded:
The worst-kept secret is out: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced is coming out in 2026. This is amazing news for long-time AC fans, but it comes with a caveat if you love the Nintendo Switch or Switch 2.

Everything we’ve seen of Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced is incredible. Ubisoft has done a great job making a 13-year-old game look brand new. It’s coming to all major platforms and consoles, except for either of Nintendo‘s main hardware, sadly.

Nintendo Switch Missing Out On Black Flag Resynced Pirate Party

It’s time for a sad sea shanty, as Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced will not be releasing on the Nintendo Switch or Switch 2 on July 9, 2026.

The Ubisoft store listing for the game clearly states the platforms, and none of the Switch devices are included.

It’s unclear if this is a hardware issue and the Switch 2 not being able to run the game, which would be odd as AC Shadows runs and plays on the Switch 2, or if it’s something else.

This is speculation on my part, but a future Switch 2 port shouldn’t be ruled out.

Practically all Assassin’s Creed games have eventually launched on the Switch and Switch 2, so don’t go keelhauling yourself over this disappointment just yet!

Which version of Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced will you be buying? Do you think you’ll stump up some extra gold for the alluring Collector’s Edition? Let us know via the Insider Gaming Discord.

Also, for more on Resynced, make sure you read other popular stories:

  • Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Features Reworked Animus With New Moments
  • Resynced Showcase Confirms Lack of DLC And Multiplayer
  • Edward Kenway’s Voice Actor Returns For Black Flag Resynced
  • Don’t Worry, Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Isn’t An RPG
  • Revamped Tailing Missions Shown During AC Black Flag Resynced Showcase
  • Black Flag Resynced Combat ‘Reworked To An Action-Oriented Experience’

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Invincible VS Story Details & Character Pass Revealed
item.content:encoded:
New story details have been revealed for Invincible VS, showcasing an original story unique to the game, from the same team behind the show.

As if that wasn’t exciting enough, the first two characters for the Year 1 Character Pass have also been revealed, as Universa and The Immortal will be the first to join the base roster.

Invincible VS Offers A Unique Story

Until now, news regarding the game’s story mode had been scarce. Focus had been on the gameplay itself, which features 3v3 tag fighting and incredibly gory combat.

Well, the first trailer for the campaign has now been shared online, and it teases an entirely unique story separate from the show and comics. It’s clear that it will revolve around the Viltrumites invading Earth, with the heroes from across the planet desperate to stop them.

At the centre of the conflict is Omni Man, who appears dead set on conquering Earth with his Viltrumite companions—a clear departure from the show’s narrative. Expect Conquest and Anissa to face off against Bulletproof and Rex Splode, as uneven as those matchups might sound!

Once you’re done with the campaign and have mastered the base roster of 18 characters, you can look forward to new arrivals with the Character Pass. Arriving this Summer will be both Universa and The Immortal, with two more set to arrive before the year closes: one in the Fall, and the other in Winter.

Considering that it explicitly mentions Year One Character Pass, it should be safe to assume that more Passes will arrive in the future. With a character line-up as vast as Invincible’s, it’s fair to say they could add new fighters for years to come. Check out the trailer for yourself down below.

Are you going to be playing the Invincible VS campaign or largely focusing on multiplayer battles? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced not being an RPG, as well as everything announced during the Arpil 2026 ID@Xbox Showcase.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: France Confirm Esports Nations Cup LoL Roster Without Hans Sama
item.content:encoded:
France has confirmed its intended League of Legends roster for the Esports Nations Cup 2026 and is just awaiting approval from the Esports World Cup Foundation.

In an interview with RFT, Karmine Corp assistant coach and LoL France coach Quentin ‘Zeph’ Viguié confirmed his roster choices.

The League of Legends event is expected to feature 32 teams, 16 directly invited nations and another 16 that will have to qualify.

According to RFT, France is expected to be one of the invited nations.

The LoL roster France has submitted is:

  • Top lane — Adam ‘Adam’ Maanane
  • Jungle — Rudy ‘SkewMond’ Semaan
  • Mid — Ilias ‘nuc’ Bizriken
  • Bot lane — Caliste ‘Caliste’ Henry-Hennebert
  • Support — Théo ‘Zoelys’ Le Scornec

The star of this team is SkewMond, who stepped up this year and helped G2 Esports restore faith for Western LoL fans at First Stand with strong performances against some of the world’s best junglers.

Meanwhile, Caliste is a controversial choice over SkewMond’s G2 teammate, Steven ‘Hans Sama’ Liv.

Zeph claims it was a very close decision, but in the end opted for the player he already works with at Karmine Corp.

“Seeing SkewMond with Hans is something we already know from G2. Seeing Skewmond with Caliste — we haven’t seen that yet, and we want to,” he told RFT.

Zoelys was also a somewhat surprising choice given that Paul ‘Stend’ Lardin has recently competed in the LEC with Team Heretics.

EMEA Masters Winter 2026 finalist Zoelys struggled during his LEC stint with Rogue, but was the preferred choice of both Caliste and Hans Sama, according to Zeph.

The team’s solo lanes are a slight question mark, with Adam currently not on a pro team and Nuc out of form with Shifters struggling in the LEC.

However, Adam is the only active French toplaner with international experience, and Shifters’ form isn’t necessarily down to their mid laner.

How strong do you think this France line-up is and what other rosters are you excited for? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And along with Zeph, hundreds of Esports Nations Cup coaches have been confirmed in preparation for the event in November.

item.title: ID@Xbox Showcase April 2026—Everything Announced
item.content:encoded:
It’s springtime, and that means showcase season is upon us, this time with the ID@Xbox Showcase. On Thursday, April 26, Xbox held its ID@Xbox showcase for April 2026 in partnership with IGN.

This is the first ID@Xbox Showcase since October 2025. That show featured games like Mouse P.I. For Hire, Invincible VS, Planet of Lana 2, and 33 Immortals, among others.

The broadcast started at 1pm ET/10am PT, and featured indie games like Aphelion, Misfall Hunter, Solo Leveling Arise Overdrive, and much more. There’s even been teases of a “surprise” coming during the event for gamers.

  • More Insider Gaming: Galaxies Showcase Spring 2026—Everything Announced

You can check out everything that was announced during the event below. Be sure to let us know your thoughts on the showcase in the comments, or join this and more discussions in the official Insider Gaming Discord. It’s worth noting that everything is in the order it was shown.

Everything Announced At The ID@Xbox Showcase April 2026

As games are shown, they will be updated below in the order they appeared. If something is missing, please let us know in the comments. Trailers will also be added as they become available.

There Are No Ghosts At The Grand

Vapor World

Mistfall Hunter

Starseeker Astroneer Expeditions

Aphelion

Vampire Crawlers

For even more upcoming games, check out everything that was announced during the recent Triple-I Showcase. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Don’t Worry, Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Isn’t An RPG
item.content:encoded:
During the Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Showcase, Ubisoft announced that the game will not be an RPG, despite previous rumors.

In a direct response to a question over what Black Flag Resynced actually is, it was said, “This remains a solo adventure and character-driven experience. It is not an RPG.”

Overhauls Galore

Despite the game remaining a solo adventure, Black Flag Resynced comes with a plethora of upgrades, including enhancing underwater exploration, a new weather system that will affect your ship’s movement, and more.

In addition, Black Flag Resynced will also include new character missions for three brand-new crewmates, additional content for Edward Kenway, and more.

They were simpler times indeed. Times change, though, and as we’ve come to learn with Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced, the combat will be updated, just like the modern-day Animus has, and as have tailing/eavesdropping missions.

Through a leaked Resynced presentation, we’ve been able to gather key details about the remake, including Edward Kenway’s original voice actor returning for the role.

The full updates can be seen in the Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Showcase here.

Will you be buying Assassin’s Creed Resynced? Let us know what you think via the free-to-join and always lively Insider Gaming Discord.

We have more Resynced content from the Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Collector’s Edition goodies being revealed, to the news that Resynced doesn’t include DLC or Multiplayer.

Tom Henderson is Insider Gaming's Co-Founder and Editor-In-Chief. When he's not running one of the industry's leading independent video game websites, he's probably playing an FPS like Call of Duty,…

More from Tom Henderson
